findings.description: Hierarchical numbered list structure lost - all sub-items rendered as flat numbered lists starting with '1.' instead of using proper roman numeral (i, ii, iii) and letter (A, B, C) sub-numbering
findings.evidence: PDF shows proper hierarchical numbering like (1)(a)(i)(A)(B), (ii), etc. HTML renders all items as '1.' flat lists, losing the hierarchical distinction between levels

findings.description: The deeply nested hierarchical outline structure of the regulation is flattened in the HTML. Multi-level indentation showing the relationship between sections, subsections, and sub-subsections is lost.
findings.evidence: PDF shows clear indentation levels: (1) -> (a) -> (i) -> (A). HTML collapses these into a less structured format with inconsistent indentation and flat numbering.

findings.description: HTML comments (<!--OL_TYPE_i-->) are visible as rendered text in the HTML version
findings.evidence: PDF has no such markup artifacts. HTML shows '<!--OL_TYPE_i-->' as visible text before list sections.

findings.description: Section (1)(b) Broad Creek content breaks out of the structured list format in HTML and appears as unformatted body text outside the list structure
findings.evidence: PDF shows (b) Broad Creek as a properly indented sub-item under (1). HTML renders it as standalone paragraph text outside the list structure.

findings.description: HTML comments <!--OL_TYPE_i--> visible as rendered text throughout the HTML document
findings.evidence: PDF has no such artifacts. HTML displays these raw HTML comments as visible text in multiple locations.

findings.description: Middletown Creek section (c) breaks out of the structured list in HTML - partially rendered as structured list item and partially as unstructured text
findings.evidence: PDF shows (c) Middletown Creek as a properly indented item under (2). HTML splits this across different formatting contexts.

findings.description: Numbered sub-items under Slade Creek use flat '1.' numbering instead of proper roman numerals (i) through (vii)
findings.evidence: PDF shows (i) Upper Slade Creek, (ii) Jarvis Creek, etc. HTML shows all as '1.' items

findings.description: Sub-items under Fortescue Creek use flat '1.' numbering instead of roman numerals (i) through (viii)
findings.evidence: PDF shows (i) Headwaters, (ii) Warner Creek, etc. HTML shows all as '1.' items

findings.description: Section transitions between major areas (Pungo River Area items) lose their hierarchical structure with items like Flax Pond (c) and Battalina (d) appearing at inconsistent structural levels
findings.evidence: PDF maintains clear (a)(b)(c)(d) structure under (3). HTML breaks structure between pages.

findings.description: Goose Creek sub-items lose proper roman numeral numbering throughout
findings.evidence: PDF shows (i) Hatter Creek, (ii) Upper Spring Creek with (A)(B) sub-items, (iii) Eastham Creek, etc. HTML uses flat '1.' numbering

findings.description: Broad Creek section (7)(b) sub-items rendered with flat numbering losing complex hierarchical structure
findings.evidence: PDF shows items (i) through (xii) with proper roman numerals under (b) Broad Creek. HTML renders all as '1.' flat list items.

findings.description: Sanders Creek coordinates show encoding issues - degree symbols appear as special characters
findings.evidence: PDF shows clean '34° 42.4694' format. HTML shows '34Â° 42.4694' or similar encoding artifacts for the degree symbol in some locations.

findings.description: New River section (13)(c) sub-items all rendered as flat '1.' lists instead of proper roman numerals (i) through (xv)
findings.evidence: PDF shows (i) Trap's Bay, (ii) Courthouse Bay, etc. HTML shows all as '1.' items

findings.description: Chadwick Bay section (d) and its sub-items break out of proper list structure in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows (d) Chadwick Bay as a properly nested item under (13)(c). HTML renders it partially outside the structured list.

findings.description: The HTML completely fails to render the deeply nested hierarchical structure of the regulation. The document uses a complex nested numbered/lettered outline (1-22 at top level, a-r at second level, i-xv at third level, A-D at fourth level), but the HTML renders this as a broken mix of ordered lists, unordered lists, preformatted code blocks, and HTML comments like <!--OL_TYPE_i--> instead of proper nested list structures.
findings.evidence: The PDF has clear hierarchical structure: (1)→(a)→(i)→(A). The HTML uses code blocks (<pre><code>) with HTML comments like <!--OL_TYPE_i--> and <!--OL_TYPE_a--> embedded as plain text, rendering the outline structure nearly unreadable and structurally broken.

findings.description: In section (16)(a), the HTML changes 'Channel)' to 'Channel.' altering punctuation that affects readability of the exception clause.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'Butler's Creek (Utley's Channel) from the IWW to Nixon's Channel'. HTML: 'Butler's Creek (Utley's Channel. from the IWW to Nixon's Channel' - closing parenthesis changed to period.

findings.description: HTML comments containing structural hints like <!--OL_TYPE_i--> and <!--OL_TYPE_a--> appear as visible text artifacts in the rendered HTML. While not fabricated content per se, they are non-PDF artifacts injected into the content stream.
findings.evidence: Multiple instances of '<!--OL_TYPE_i-->' and '<!--OL_TYPE_a-->' appear throughout the HTML body, which do not exist in the source PDF.

findings.description: All 22 top-level sections and their subsections appear to be present in the HTML, though the structural rendering makes verification difficult. No whole sections appear to be missing.
findings.evidence: Sections (1) through (22) are all represented, including the History Note at the end.
